stimulus: After the invention of movable type in the late 1400s, many earlier handwritten manuscripts in Europe were dismantled and their parchment reused. In one case, pages ______blank a volume of Latin hymns from 1180 were found stitched into the binding of a later book.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between a preposition and its complement. No punctuation is needed between the preposition "from" and its complement "a volume of Latin hymns from 1180." The complement completes the meaning of the preposition, and any punctuation between the two results in an ungrammatical sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the preposition and its complement. Choice B is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the preposition and its complement. Choice C is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the preposition and its complement.
